subject: Re: Recovery after bad M run?
body:
Update: I guess the obvious thing to do is to make a  backup of the 
"average" folder....

Matthias Vorländer schrieb am Mittwoch, 25. August 2021 um 14:52:30 UTC+2:

> Dear all,
>
> while the problem of bad M runs have been described here before, I am 
> interested in how to best revert these changes.
>
> Here is what I did:
>
> Picked particles in Warp, exported subtomos, classfied and refined in 
> Relion, downgraded the star file, created a refinement run in M. This 
> decreased the resolution moderately from 23 to 25 Angstrom. However, if I 
> now go back to Warp and reconstruct full tomograms using deconvolution, the 
> tomograms look dramatically worse then before the M run. 
>
> I had this issue before, and fixed it by deleting all xml files and 
> re-running pre-processing of the raw frames and then CTF estimation of the 
> tomograms. So this time, before the M run, I created a backup of all .xml 
> files and of all .tomostar files, as well as the previous.settings files. 
> However, if i restore these after the bad M run, the reconstructed 
> tomograms still look bad.  I also tried to copy the xml files from the 
> versions folder before the M run, but the result is the same. 
>
> So, I wonder what the best way is to return to a pre-M state when things 
> go bad without having to do pre-processing again?
>
> Thanks in advance,
> Best,
> Matthias
>
